born.place: unknown — possibly Texas (if second-generation German) or Germany

surname_origin.macker_geographic_distribution: Northern/Eastern Germany — Pomerania, East Prussia, Silesia most common
surname_origin.confidence: POSSIBLE (no direct source found for Macker distribution)
surname_origin.note: Macker appears as a distinct surname in Lavaca County; possibly second-generation Texas German

immigration.confidence: PROBABLE (immigration window calculated from Herman's 1863 TX birth)

born.place: Germany or possibly Texas (if she was already born here)

province_assessment.rationale: Breslau TX in Lavaca County is named after the Prussian city of Breslau (Wroclaw), capital of Silesia; Böhme surname most concentrated in Silesia/Saxony border region; TSHA confirms postbellum German wave came from eastern provinces; Lutheran affiliation consistent with Silesian German Protestants
province_assessment.alternative_rationale: Böhme surname common in Saxony; Jakob Böhme from Görlitz (Saxony-Silesia border); LCMS roots in Saxon Lutheranism
provinces_excluded: Westphalia — wrong immigration wave and zone
Nassau/Holstein/Oldenburg — Adelsverein wave, Hill Country settlements, not Lavaca County
Bavaria — Catholic-dominant, no Bavaria markers in Lavaca County
Bohemia — Herman was ethnic German Lutheran, not Czech/Bohemian

breslau_tx.location: 7 miles NW of Hallettsville, north central Lavaca County
breslau_tx.named_after: Prussian city of Breslau, now Wroclaw, Poland (capital of Silesia)
breslau_tx.settled_by: German immigrants during and after Civil War

settlement_timeline.1850: No Boehme in Lavaca County census — family not yet arrived
settlement_timeline.1855_1862_probable: Boehme family arrives, probably via Indianola port
settlement_timeline.1863_06_09: Herman F. Boehme born in Texas — confirms parents already here
settlement_timeline.1865_1875: German immigrants including Boehme family farm in Lavaca County; Breslau, Wied, Witting communities develop
settlement_timeline.1875: Indianola hurricane destroys port; Galveston becomes primary entry point
settlement_timeline.1887: San Antonio and Aransas Pass Railway reaches Shiner area; town of Shiner formally established
settlement_timeline.1888: Frieda Boehme born — first confirmed Boehme birth record in the family
settlement_timeline.1947_05_29: Minna Marie Macker Luedecke dies age ~80-85

immigration_route.texas_entry_port: Indianola, Calhoun County, Texas (most probable for pre-1875 arrival)
immigration_route.route_inland: Old Indianola Trail → Yorktown (DeWitt County) → Lavaca County
immigration_route.source: TSHA Yorktown TX and Indianola TX entries
